reason: The text is Exodus 14, describing the crossing of the Red Sea. While the Israelite people complain to Moses in fear ('What have we done?'), Moses (the Hebrew leader) responds with encouragement, telling them 'Do not fear' and assuring them that God will fight for them. The only leaders shown with a change of heart against people are the Egyptians ('Pharaoh and his servants turned their hearts against the people'), not Hebrew leaders. Moses demonstrates leadership through reassurance and faith, not rage.
